reviews.review_msg: Got a “C” on Portfolio after requesting grade separation due to misunderstandings between group members and I. Makes me wonder if he evaluated Portfolio based on rubric criteria. Emphasized teamwork in the email where he responded to concern about Portfolio grade + final grade, but ignored the following emails.
Vague expectations. Does not post Slides. Ignores emails.
Rated him hilarious not because of his personality but because of how he grades and acts. AVOID AT ALL COST.

His syllabus was not clear on what he actually wants, and he assumes that all education classes operate the same way. He also has a lot of "hidden" rubrics where you need to ask him in person about what he wants, and he will lists a few things then. I would recommend taking this class with a different professor.

Does not understand the material he teaches, evident by his inability to lead class discussions and answer questions about basic reading ideas. Teaches educational technology but he isn't tech savvy nor a good educator. Final project is about designing a social learning web page for a learning community, which felt like an outdated concept.

This instructor is unresponsive and ignores student requests. He will not even share session slides.  They are poorly written anyway. Very negative experience. Would highly recommend you wait until another prof is teaching. Many other profs at SFU are great.
The worst professor I've had. Provides very little feedback to students. Marking is done very late. Not accessible. Not responsive.
The most boring class with zero learning. Didn't feel engaged in class at all. There are not many options at SFU but do a favour to yourself and choose someone else.

If you participate in class and do your assigned readings on time, you will be fine in this course. There is two reflection papers, a group presentation, a group portfolio, and online weekly discussions. He made the classes more interactive as we did group discussions and debates. As long as you follow guidelines, you will be graded quite fairly.
